title: Salad Shirazi (Persian Chopped Salad)
description: A crisp, refreshing, and bright salad featuring finely diced cucumbers, tomatoes, and red onions, dressed simply with citrus and dried mint.

instructions.text: Wash the Persian cucumbers, firm tomatoes, and red onion. Finely dice them into uniform pieces, about the size of a pea. Place them in a large serving bowl.
Sprinkle the dried mint evenly over the diced vegetables.
Drizzle the freshly squeezed lime juice and olive oil over the salad.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Toss gently to combine all ingredients thoroughly. For best results, let it rest in the refrigerator for 15-30 minutes before serving to let the flavors meld.

tips: The secret to an authentic Salad Shirazi is the fine, uniform dice of all the vegetables. Use a sharp knife. If using regular cucumbers, peel and deseed them first.
storage_notes: Best eaten fresh the same day, but can be stored in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 day. It will release water as it sits.
serve_with: Almost any Persian main course, including Kebabs, Baghali Polo, and Lubia Polo.

substitutions.note: Verjuice is very traditional and gives an authentic sharp tartness.
White onion works well, though red provides better color.
